name: Staging Deployment Workflow
description: Verify the skill correctly guides a full staging deployment: build, synth, diff, deploy, and smoke test.
input: Deploy my Next.js app to staging using CDK. The app is in the current directory and the CDK code is in ./cdk/. The staging config is already set up.
expected_behavior: Runs 'npm run build' to build the Next.js application
Runs 'npm install' in the CDK directory
Runs 'cdk synth -c stage=staging' to synthesize the stack
Runs 'cdk diff -c stage=staging' to preview changes
Runs 'cdk deploy -c stage=staging' to deploy
Runs smoke tests against the staging URL
Tags the deployment in git with a deploy-staging-* tag
Does NOT proceed to production without explicit approval
pass_criteria: Agent follows the staged deployment workflow, deploys only to staging, runs smoke tests, and does not auto-promote to production.

name: Production Rollback Procedure
description: Verify the skill correctly guides a production rollback when a deployment causes errors.
input: My production deployment is returning 502 errors after the latest deploy. I need to roll back immediately. The app uses ECS Fargate.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as a rollback scenario, not a new deployment
Presents rollback strategy options (CDK redeploy, ECS task swap, CFN rollback, CloudFront invalidation)
Recommends ECS task definition swap for fastest recovery given 502 errors
Provides the correct aws ecs commands to list task definitions and update the service
Includes verification steps after rollback (check service stability, CloudWatch alarms)
Mentions the CDK redeploy strategy as a follow-up for a more permanent fix
pass_criteria: Agent recommends the fastest rollback strategy (ECS swap) for a 502 error, provides correct commands, and includes post-rollback verification.

name: Environment Configuration Setup
description: Verify the skill correctly guides setting up environment-specific CDK configurations for a new project.
input: I'm setting up CDK deployment for a new Next.js project. I need staging and production configs. Staging should use minimal resources, production needs auto-scaling. The app connects to a PostgreSQL database.
expected_behavior: References the environment config template from references/
Creates staging config with minimal resources (low CPU, memory, desiredCount=1)
Creates production config with higher resources and auto-scaling (minCount >= 2, maxCount >= 4)
Uses SSM Parameter Store references for the database URL (not hardcoded)
Sets different domains for staging vs production
Includes monitoring config with stricter thresholds for production
Explains the EnvironmentConfig interface and its fields
pass_criteria: Agent creates properly differentiated staging and production configs with secure secret handling and appropriate resource sizing.
